blocks.body: Future Forms — will, be going to, Future Continuous, Future Perfect, and Future Perfect Continuous for decisions, plans, predictions, actions in progress, completed results, and duration before a future point.

{ "title": "What to pay attention to", "items": [ "If the decision arose right now, we usually choose will.", "If the plan was before the moment of speech, we usually choose be going to.", "If it is an opinion or assumption without direct evidence, we often use will.", "If there is visible evidence now, we often use be going to.", "If it matters that the action will be in progress at a future moment, look to Future Continuous.", "If a completed result before a future point matters most, look to Future Perfect.", "If it matters that the action will already have been continuing for some time by a future point and answers how long?, look to Future Perfect Continuous." ] }
